demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents; bedbound and fully dependent; a community nurse visits as part of a home palliative-care setup

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: He is turning blue, his chest is heaving, and we can't clear the thick glue from his throat. Please help — the suction machine isn't keeping up and his lips have gone dark blue.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last two hours his breathing has got much worse very fast, with lots of thick secretions he can't cough up, and now his lips are blue and he looks exhausted.

symptom_narrative.onset: started about 2 hours ago and got dramatically worse over the last hour
symptom_narrative.character: a sudden flood of thick, sticky secretions in the throat that he cannot clear, with very fast, laboured breathing; his chest sucks in and there is a tugging at the base of his throat
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst breathing episode we have seen — he looks like he is running out of steam
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and worsening since it began
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: the home suction machine helps for a moment but cannot keep up with how thick it is; a dose of his usual PRN drying medicine an hour ago did not help
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it keeps building up no matter what we do
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he feels hot to touch, he is very floppy and drowsy, and his lips and around his mouth have gone dark blue
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he gets chest infections often and has been hospitalised for them before, but this came on faster and looks worse than usual
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is bedbound and fully dependent for everything at the best of times; right now he is barely responding

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: an inherited lysosomal storage disease (an enzyme problem) that has been getting steadily worse
past_medical_history.how_managed: specialist metabolic team and home palliative-oriented care

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: he has lost the movement skills he used to have and is now very floppy, sometimes stiff

past_medical_history.how_managed: specialist follow-up; makes his breathing shallow

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a big tummy — we were told his liver and spleen are enlarged
epilepsy that is hard to control
past_medical_history.how_managed: regular anti-seizure medicines; no seizure today
repeated courses of antibiotics and hospital admissions

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a dissolve-under-the-tongue medicine to dry up secretions (hyoscine), given when needed

family_history_as_reported.narrative: the inherited enzyme condition runs in the family / was said to be genetic

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: young child, fully dependent, cared for at home with a community nurse and both parents present; advance care directive / complex care records are kept at home

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: progressive loss of previously acquired motor skills; non-verbal with profound cognitive impairment; fully dependent

objective_data_offered.value: oxygen monitor at home is reading 82%
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: home fingertip oximeter on a cold, poorly-perfused, very unwell child — reading may under- or over-read, but a value this low with visible cyanosis is deeply concerning

objective_data_offered.value: the same monitor shows his heart rate around 165
he is breathing really fast — we can't count it, and his chest and the notch at the base of his neck are sucking in with every breath, with flaring nostrils
dark blue discolouration of the lips and around the mouth; very fast shallow breathing; child limp and barely rousable; loud rattly noisy breathing from secretions
home suction machine is running continuously but struggling with how thick and sticky the secretions are
